diagrams.title: Capacity Utilisation vs Performance Curve
diagrams.description: Shows the relationship between system utilisation and performance response time
elements: Idle State (0-30%): Low utilisation, good performance but wasteful
Optimal Zone (30-75%): Best balance of utilization and performance
Warning Zone (75-85%): Declining performance, alerts should trigger
Critical Zone (85-100%): Severe degradation, potential failures
diagrams.purpose: Teaches why optimal utilization is around 70-80% - leaving headroom for traffic spikes while avoiding over-provisioning costs

diagrams.title: Capacity Planning Types Timeline
diagrams.description: Horizontal timeline showing three capacity planning types
elements: Far Left: Operational Planning (Days-Weeks) - Daily monitoring, immediate adjustments
Middle: Tactical Planning (6-18 Months) - Seasonal planning, project-based capacity
Far Right: Strategic Planning (1-3 Years) - Business growth, major infrastructure changes
diagrams.purpose: Clarifies that different planning horizons require different approaches and detail levels

diagrams.title: Capacity Planning Process Cycle
diagrams.description: Circular flowchart showing continuous capacity management cycle
elements: Monitor (collect metrics) → Analyze (identify trends) → Plan (forecast needs) → Implement (deploy resources) → Review (measure effectiveness) → returns to Monitor
diagrams.purpose: Shows capacity planning as ongoing iterative process, not one-time activity

diagrams.title: Vertical vs Horizontal Scaling Comparison
diagrams.description: Side-by-side visual comparison of scaling approaches
elements: Vertical: Single server icon with upward arrow, showing more CPU/RAM added to same box
Horizontal: Multiple server icons with plus sign, showing more servers added in cluster
Trade-off labels: Vertical = simpler but limited; Horizontal = complex but highly scalable
diagrams.purpose: Clarifies the two fundamental scaling strategies and when to use each
